item.title: Eli Lilly's Foundayo (Retatrutide)
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly's Foundayo (retatrutide) demonstrates positive heart safety data in trials, easing FDA concerns and paving the way for a diabetes filing. The GLP-1/GIP/glucagon triple agonist shows efficacy in obesity and diabetes management, with plans for parallel submissions.
  • MoAs: GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptor triple agonist that enhances insulin secretion, reduces appetite, and improves metabolic control.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, BioSpace, Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News

item.title: GLP-1 / Weight Loss Drugs
item.description:
• Summary: New approaches in GLP-1-based weight loss drugs are explored, highlighting ongoing innovation in this therapeutic area to improve efficacy and safety for obesity and diabetes management.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 receptor agonists that mimic incretin hormones to reduce appetite and slow gastric emptying, leading to weight loss and improved glycemic control.
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: Travere Therapeutics Kidney Disease Drug
item.description:
• Summary: Travere Therapeutics receives FDA approval for its kidney disease drug, sparsentan, marking a milestone for patients with conditions like IgA nephropathy.
  • MoAs: Sparsentan: Dual endothelin and angiotensin II receptor antagonist (DEARA), reducing proteinuria and slowing disease progression.
  • Sources: STAT News
